insight: REFLECT declaring stable on iter1 is always a vote in disguise -- there is nothing to compare to on the first iteration. Minimum 2 iterations before stable verdict is valid. When you see iter1->stable, force a second SYNTHESIZE pass.

CHAIN-SELECT heuristic catches explicit-verb short intents (what is X) but misses compare/decide intents (how do I decide between X and Y) which look like simple questions but span decision + design. Add explicit check: if intent contains decide/compare/choose/whether, default to full chain regardless of word count.

The GROUND recovery option (re-deepen once) only helps when the gap is FRAMING (problem mis-scoped) not EVIDENCE (user has not provided data). For evidence gaps, re-deepening produces the same UNGROUNDED result and wastes one recovery attempt. Detect gap type before triggering recovery: framing gap -> re-deepen; evidence gap -> ask 3 targeted questions directly.
